<content>1: // ─── SSE Event Wiring ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Extracted from relay-stack.ts: the pipeline that takes SSE events from
3: // OpenCode, translates them, filters by session, records to cache, broadcasts
4: // to browser clients, and sends push notifications.
5: 
6: import type { Translator } from "./event-translator.js";
7: import type { MessageCache } from "./message-cache.js";
8: import {
9: 	hasInfoWithSessionID,
10: 	hasPartWithSessionID,
11: 	hasSessionID,
12: 	isPermissionRepliedEvent,
13: 	isSessionErrorEvent,
14: } from "./opencode-events.js";
15: import type { PermissionBridge } from "./permission-bridge.js";
16: import type { PushNotificationManager } from "./push.js";
17: import type { QuestionBridge } from "./question-bridge.js";
18: import type { SessionManager } from "./session-manager.js";
19: import type { SessionOverrides } from "./session-overrides.js";
20: import type { SSEConsumer } from "./sse-consumer.js";
21: import type { OpenCodeEvent, RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
22: 
23: // ─── Session ID extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
24: // OpenCode SSE events store sessionID in different locations by event type:
25: //   - Top-level: message.part.delta, session.status, message.part.removed, etc.
26: //   - Nested in part: message.part.updated → properties.part.sessionID
27: //   - Nested in info: message.updated → properties.info.sessionID
28: // We must check all locations to correctly attribute events to sessions.
29: 
30: export function extractSessionId(event: OpenCodeEvent): string | undefined {
31: 	const props = event.properties;
32: 	// 1. Top-level sessionID (most common)
33: 	if (hasSessionID(props)) {
34: 		return props.sessionID;
35: 	}
36: 	// 2. Nested in part (message.part.updated)
37: 	if (hasPartWithSessionID(props)) {
38: 		return props.part.sessionID;
39: 	}
40: 	// 3. Nested in info (message.updated, session.updated)
41: 	if (hasInfoWithSessionID(props)) {
42: 		return props.info.sessionID ?? props.info.id;
43: 	}
44: 	return undefined;
45: }
46: 
47: // ─── Cache-eligible event types ──────────────────────────────────────────────
48: // Only chat-relevant events should be recorded to the message cache.
49: // Non-chat events (file_changed, permission_*, todo_state, etc.) would create
50: // false-positive cache hits that skip the REST API fallback on session switch,
51: // causing pre-relay conversation history to be lost.
52: 
53: const CACHEABLE_TYPES = new Set([
54: 	"user_message",
55: 	"delta",
56: 	"thinking_start",
57: 	"thinking_delta",
58: 	"thinking_stop",
59: 	"tool_start",
60: 	"tool_executing",
61: 	"tool_result",
62: 	"result",
63: 	"status",
64: 	"done",
65: 	"error",
66: ]);
67: 
68: export function isCacheable(msg: { type: string }): boolean {
69: 	return CACHEABLE_TYPES.has(msg.type);
70: }
71: 
72: // ─── SSE Wiring Dependencies ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
73: 
74: export interface SSEWiringDeps {
75: 	translator: Translator;
76: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
77: 	messageCache: MessageCache;
78: 	permissionBridge: PermissionBridge;
79: 	questionBridge: QuestionBridge;
80: 	overrides: SessionOverrides;
81: 	wsHandler: {
82: 		broadcast: (msg: RelayMessage) => void;
83: 	};
84: 	pushManager?: PushNotificationManager;
85: 	log: (...args: unknown[]) => void;
86: }
87: 
88: // ─── Handle a single SSE event ───────────────────────────────────────────────
89: 
90: export function handleSSEEvent(
91: 	deps: SSEWiringDeps,
92: 	event: OpenCodeEvent,
93: ): void {
94: 	const {
95: 		translator,
96: 		sessionMgr,
97: 		messageCache,
98: 		permissionBridge,
99: 		questionBridge,
100: 		overrides,
101: 		wsHandler,
102: 		pushManager,
103: 		log,
104: 	} = deps;
105: 
106: 	const eventSessionId = extractSessionId(event);
107: 	log(`   [sse] event=${event.type} session=${eventSessionId ?? "?"}`);
108: 
109: 	// ── Permission / question bridge routing ──────────────────────────────
110: 
111: 	if (event.type === "permission.asked") {
112: 		permissionBridge.onPermissionRequest(event);
113: 		if (pushManager) {
114: 			const id =
115: 				typeof event.properties.id === "string"
116: 					? event.properties.id
117: 					: "unknown";
118: 			const tool =
119: 				typeof event.properties.tool === "string"
120: 					? event.properties.tool
121: 					: typeof event.properties.permission === "string"
122: 						? event.properties.permission
123: 						: "A tool";
124: 			pushManager
125: 				.sendToAll({
126: 					type: "permission_request",
127: 					title: "Permission Needed",
128: 					body: `${tool} needs approval`,
129: 					tag: `perm-${id}`,
130: 				})
131: 				.catch(() => {});
132: 		}
133: 	}
134: 	if (event.type === "question.asked") {
135: 		questionBridge.onQuestion(event);
136: 		if (pushManager) {
137: 			pushManager
138: 				.sendToAll({
139: 					type: "ask_user",
140: 					title: "Question from Claude",
141: 					body: "Claude has a question for you",
142: 					tag: "opencode-ask",
143: 				})
144: 				.catch(() => {});
145: 		}
146: 	}
147: 	if (isPermissionRepliedEvent(event)) {
148: 		permissionBridge.onPermissionReplied(event.properties.id);
149: 	}
150: 
151: 	// ── Log session errors for debugging ──────────────────────────────────
152: 
153: 	if (isSessionErrorEvent(event)) {
154: 		const err = event.properties.error;
155: 		log(
156: 			`   [sse] event=${event.type} session=${eventSessionId ?? "?"} Session error: ${err?.name ?? "?"} — ${err?.data?.message ?? "(no message)"}`,
157: 		);
158: 	}
159: 
160: 	// ── Translate → filter → cache → broadcast ───────────────────────────
161: 
162: 	const messages = translator.translate(event);
163: 	if (!messages) return;
164: 
165: 	const activeId = sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId();
166: 	const isActiveSession = !eventSessionId || eventSessionId === activeId;
167: 
168: 	const toSend = Array.isArray(messages) ? messages : [messages];
169: 	for (const msg of toSend) {
170: 		// Clear processing timeout when done event arrives for active session
171: 		if (msg.type === "done" && isActiveSession) {
172: 			overrides.clearProcessingTimeout();
173: 		}
174: 
175: 		// Only record chat-relevant events to the cache
176: 		const recordId = eventSessionId ?? activeId;
177: 		if (recordId && isCacheable(msg)) {
178: 			messageCache.recordEvent(recordId, msg);
179: 		}
180: 
181: 		// Only broadcast events for the active session to browser clients
182: 		if (!isActiveSession) continue;
183: 		wsHandler.broadcast(msg);
184: 
185: 		// Push notifications for done/error events
186: 		if (pushManager) {
187: 			if (msg.type === "done") {
188: 				pushManager
189: 					.sendToAll({
190: 						type: "done",
191: 						title: "Task Complete",
192: 						body: "Claude has finished processing.",
193: 						tag: "opencode-done",
194: 					})
195: 					.catch(() => {});
196: 			}
197: 			if (msg.type === "error") {
198: 				pushManager
199: 					.sendToAll({
200: 						type: "error",
201: 						title: "Error",
202: 						body: msg.message || "An error occurred",
203: 						tag: "opencode-error",
204: 					})
205: 					.catch(() => {});
206: 			}
207: 		}
208: 	}
209: }
210: 
211: // ─── Wire all SSE consumer event listeners ───────────────────────────────────
212: 
213: export function wireSSEConsumer(
214: 	deps: SSEWiringDeps,
215: 	consumer: SSEConsumer,
216: ): void {
217: 	const { log } = deps;
218: 
219: 	consumer.on("connected", () => {
220: 		log(
221: 			`   [sse] session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Connected to OpenCode event stream`,
222: 		);
223: 		deps.wsHandler.broadcast({
224: 			type: "connection_status",
225: 			status: "connected",
226: 		});
227: 	});
228: 	consumer.on("disconnected", (err) => {
229: 		log(
230: 			`   [sse] session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Disconnected${err ? `: ${err.message}` : ""}`,
231: 		);
232: 		deps.wsHandler.broadcast({
233: 			type: "connection_status",
234: 			status: "disconnected",
235: 		});
236: 	});
237: 	consumer.on("reconnecting", ({ attempt, delay }) => {
238: 		log(
239: 			`   [sse] session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Reconnecting (attempt ${attempt}, ${delay}ms delay)…`,
240: 		);
241: 		deps.wsHandler.broadcast({
242: 			type: "connection_status",
243: 			status: "reconnecting",
244: 		});
245: 	});
246: 	consumer.on("error", (err) =>
247: 		log(
248: 			`   [sse] session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Error: ${err.message}`,
249: 		),
250: 	);
251: 
252: 	consumer.on("event", (event: OpenCodeEvent) => {
253: 		handleSSEEvent(deps, event);
254: 	});
255: }

(End of file - total 255 lines)
</content>

<content>1: // ─── WebSocket Message Router (Ticket 2.2) ──────────────────────────────────
2: // Pure logic for routing incoming WebSocket messages to correct handlers,
3: // client tracking, broadcast targeting, and state snapshot building.
4: // Deliberately IO-free: no actual WebSocket I/O — just routing decisions.
5: 
6: import type { RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
7: 
8: // ─── Message types from browser ──────────────────────────────────────────────
9: 
10: export type IncomingMessageType =
11: 	| "message"
12: 	| "permission_response"
13: 	| "ask_user_response"
14: 	| "question_reject"
15: 	| "new_session"
16: 	| "switch_session"
17: 	| "delete_session"
18: 	| "rename_session"
19: 	| "list_sessions"
20: 	| "search_sessions"
21: 	| "load_more_history"
22: 	| "terminal_command"
23: 	| "input_sync"
24: 	| "switch_agent"
25: 	| "switch_model"
26: 	| "get_todo"
27: 	| "get_agents"
28: 	| "get_models"
29: 	| "get_commands"
30: 	| "get_projects"
31: 	| "add_project"
32: 	| "get_file_list"
33: 	| "get_file_content"
34: 	| "pty_create"
35: 	| "pty_input"
36: 	| "pty_resize"
37: 	| "pty_close"
38: 	| "cancel"
39: 	| "rewind";
40: 
41: const VALID_MESSAGE_TYPES = new Set<string>([
42: 	"message",
43: 	"permission_response",
44: 	"ask_user_response",
45: 	"question_reject",
46: 	"new_session",
47: 	"switch_session",
48: 	"delete_session",
49: 	"rename_session",
50: 	"list_sessions",
51: 	"search_sessions",
52: 	"load_more_history",
53: 	"terminal_command",
54: 	"input_sync",
55: 	"switch_agent",
56: 	"switch_model",
57: 	"get_todo",
58: 	"get_agents",
59: 	"get_models",
60: 	"get_commands",
61: 	"get_projects",
62: 	"add_project",
63: 	"get_file_list",
64: 	"get_file_content",
65: 	"pty_create",
66: 	"pty_input",
67: 	"pty_resize",
68: 	"pty_close",
69: 	"cancel",
70: 	"rewind",
71: ]);
72: 
73: export interface IncomingMessage {
74: 	type: string;
75: 	[key: string]: unknown;
76: }
77: 
78: export interface RouteResult {
79: 	handler: IncomingMessageType;
80: 	payload: Record<string, unknown>;
81: }
82: 
83: export interface ErrorResult {
84: 	type: "error";
85: 	code: string;
86: 	message: string;
87: }
88: 
89: // ─── Message routing ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
90: 
91: /**
92:  * Parse and validate an incoming WebSocket message.
93:  * Returns the parsed object or null if parsing fails.
94:  */
95: export function parseIncomingMessage(raw: string): IncomingMessage | null {
96: 	try {
97: 		const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
98: 		if (
99: 			typeof parsed !== "object" ||
100: 			parsed === null ||
101: 			Array.isArray(parsed)
102: 		) {
103: 			return null;
104: 		}
105: 		if (typeof parsed.type !== "string") {
106: 			return null;
107: 		}
108: 		return parsed as IncomingMessage;
109: 	} catch {
110: 		return null;
111: 	}
112: }
113: 
114: /**
115:  * Route a parsed message to its handler.
116:  * Returns either a RouteResult or an ErrorResult for unknown types.
117:  */
118: export function routeMessage(msg: IncomingMessage): RouteResult | ErrorResult {
119: 	if (!VALID_MESSAGE_TYPES.has(msg.type)) {
120: 		return {
121: 			type: "error",
122: 			code: "UNKNOWN_MESSAGE_TYPE",
123: 			message: `Unknown message type: ${msg.type}`,
124: 		};
125: 	}
126: 
127: 	const { type, ...payload } = msg;
128: 	return {
129: 		handler: type as IncomingMessageType,
130: 		payload,
131: 	};
132: }
133: 
134: /**
135:  * Check if a route result is an error.
136:  */
137: export function isRouteError(
138: 	result: RouteResult | ErrorResult,
139: ): result is ErrorResult {
140: 	return "type" in result && (result as ErrorResult).type === "error";
141: }
142: 
143: // ─── Client tracking ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
144: 
145: export interface ClientTracker {
146: 	addClient(clientId: string): number;
147: 	removeClient(clientId: string): number;
148: 	getClientCount(): number;
149: 	getClientIds(): string[];
150: 	hasClient(clientId: string): boolean;
151: 	getBroadcastTargets(excludeClientId?: string): string[];
152: }
153: 
154: /**
155:  * Create a client tracker for managing connected WebSocket clients.
156:  */
157: export function createClientTracker(): ClientTracker {
158: 	const clients = new Set<string>();
159: 
160: 	return {
161: 		addClient(clientId: string): number {
162: 			clients.add(clientId);
163: 			return clients.size;
164: 		},
165: 
166: 		removeClient(clientId: string): number {
167: 			clients.delete(clientId);
168: 			return clients.size;
169: 		},
170: 
171: 		getClientCount(): number {
172: 			return clients.size;
173: 		},
174: 
175: 		getClientIds(): string[] {
176: 			return [...clients];
177: 		},
178: 
179: 		hasClient(clientId: string): boolean {
180: 			return clients.has(clientId);
181: 		},
182: 
183: 		getBroadcastTargets(excludeClientId?: string): string[] {
184: 			if (!excludeClientId) return [...clients];
185: 			return [...clients].filter((id) => id !== excludeClientId);
186: 		},
187: 	};
188: }
189: 
190: // ─── State snapshot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
191: 
192: export interface SessionState {
193: 	sessionId: string;
194: 	title: string;
195: 	model?: string;
196: 	provider?: string;
197: 	status: "idle" | "processing";
198: 	clientCount: number;
199: }
200: 
201: export interface PendingState {
202: 	permissions: Array<{ requestId: string; toolName: string }>;
203: 	questions: Array<{ toolId: string; questions: unknown[] }>;
204: }
205: 
206: export interface StateSnapshot {
207: 	session: SessionState;
208: 	pending: PendingState;
209: 	clientCount: number;
210: }
211: 
212: /**
213:  * Build a state snapshot for a newly connected client.
214:  * The snapshot includes the session state, pending interactions, and client count.
215:  */
216: export function buildStateSnapshot(
217: 	session: SessionState,
218: 	pending: PendingState,
219: 	clientCount: number,
220: ): StateSnapshot {
221: 	return {
222: 		session: { ...session, clientCount },
223: 		pending: {
224: 			permissions: [...pending.permissions],
225: 			questions: [...pending.questions],
226: 		},
227: 		clientCount,
228: 	};
229: }
230: 
231: /**
232:  * Build the list of messages to send to a newly connected client.
233:  * Returns RelayMessage[] in the correct order per AC5.
234:  */
235: export function buildNewClientMessages(
236: 	snapshot: StateSnapshot,
237: ): RelayMessage[] {
238: 	const messages: RelayMessage[] = [];
239: 
240: 	// 1. Status
241: 	messages.push({
242: 		type: "status",
243: 		status: snapshot.session.status,
244: 	});
245: 
246: 	// 2. Client count
247: 	messages.push({
248: 		type: "client_count",
249: 		count: snapshot.clientCount,
250: 	});
251: 
252: 	// 3. Pending permissions
253: 	for (const perm of snapshot.pending.permissions) {
254: 		messages.push({
255: 			type: "permission_request",
256: 			requestId: perm.requestId,
257: 			toolName: perm.toolName,
258: 			toolInput: {},
259: 		});
260: 	}
261: 
262: 	// 4. Pending questions
263: 	for (const q of snapshot.pending.questions) {
264: 		messages.push({
265: 			type: "ask_user",
266: 			toolId: q.toolId,
267: 			questions: q.questions as RelayMessage extends { type: "ask_user" }
268: 				? never
269: 				: unknown[],
270: 		} as RelayMessage);
271: 	}
272: 
273: 	return messages;
274: }
275: 
276: // ─── Client count message factory ────────────────────────────────────────────
277: 
278: export function createClientCountMessage(count: number): RelayMessage {
279: 	return { type: "client_count", count };
280: }

(End of file - total 280 lines)
</content>

<content>1: // ─── Message Handlers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

3: // table. This module replaces the monolithic message-handlers.ts.

99: 	load_more_history: handleLoadMoreHistory,
100: 	get_agents: handleGetAgents,
101: 	switch_agent: handleSwitchAgent,
102: 	get_models: handleGetModels,
103: 	switch_model: handleSwitchModel,
104: 	get_commands: handleGetCommands,
105: 	get_projects: handleGetProjects,

107: 	get_file_list: handleGetFileList,
108: 	get_file_content: handleGetFileContent,
109: 	terminal_command: handleTerminalCommand,
110: 	pty_create: handlePtyCreate,
111: 	pty_input: handlePtyInput,
112: 	pty_resize: handlePtyResize,
113: 	pty_close: handlePtyClose,
114: 	cancel: handleCancel,
115: 	rewind: handleRewind,
116: 	input_sync: handleInputSync,
117: 	get_todo: handleGetTodo,

119: 
120: /**
121:  * Dispatch a client WebSocket message to the appropriate handler.
122:  * Returns without error for unrecognised handler names (logged only).
123:  */
124: export async function dispatchMessage(
125: 	deps: HandlerDeps,
126: 	clientId: string,
127: 	handler: string,
128: 	payload: Record<string, unknown>,
129: ): Promise<void> {
130: 	const fn = MESSAGE_HANDLERS[handler];
131: 	if (fn) {
132: 		await fn(deps, clientId, payload);
133: 	} else {
134: 		deps.log(
135: 			`   [ws] client=${clientId} session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Unhandled: ${handler}`,
136: 		);
137: 	}
138: }

<content>1: // ─── Relay Stack ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // The complete relay wiring: OpenCode client, SSE consumer, event translator,
3: // WebSocket handler, session manager, permission/question bridges.
4: //
5: // Extracted from skeleton.ts so integration tests exercise the exact same
6: // wiring as production. skeleton.ts is now a thin CLI wrapper around this.
7: 
8: import { stat } from "node:fs/promises";
9: import { createRequire } from "node:module";
10: import { homedir } from "node:os";
11: import { join, resolve } from "node:path";
12: import { type ClientInitDeps, handleClientConnected } from "./client-init.js";
13: import { formatErrorDetail, RelayError } from "./errors.js";
14: import { createTranslator } from "./event-translator.js";
15: import { dispatchMessage, type HandlerDeps } from "./handlers/index.js";
16: import { getClientIp, parseCookies } from "./http-utils.js";
17: import { generateSlug } from "./ipc-protocol.js";
18: import { MessageCache } from "./message-cache.js";
19: import { OpenCodeClient } from "./opencode-client.js";
20: import { PermissionBridge } from "./permission-bridge.js";
21: import { PtyManager, type PtyUpstream } from "./pty-manager.js";
22: import type { PushNotificationManager } from "./push.js";
23: import { QuestionBridge } from "./question-bridge.js";
24: import { RelayServer } from "./server.js";
25: import { SessionManager } from "./session-manager.js";
26: import { SessionOverrides } from "./session-overrides.js";
27: import { SSEConsumer } from "./sse-consumer.js";
28: import { wireSSEConsumer } from "./sse-wiring.js";
29: import type { PartType, ProjectRelayConfig, ToolStatus } from "./types.js";
30: import { WebSocketHandler } from "./ws-handler.js";
31: 
32: // ─── WebSocket library for upstream PTY connections ─────────────────────────
33: const requireWs = createRequire(import.meta.url);
34: const wsLib = requireWs("ws");
35: const WebSocketClass = wsLib.WebSocket as typeof import("ws").WebSocket;
36: type RawData = import("ws").RawData;
37: 
38: /** Per-project relay: all relay components attached to a shared server. */
39: export interface ProjectRelay {
40: 	wsHandler: WebSocketHandler;
41: 	sseConsumer: SSEConsumer;
42: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
43: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
44: 	translator: ReturnType<typeof createTranslator>;
45: 	permissionBridge: PermissionBridge;
46: 	questionBridge: QuestionBridge;
47: 	/** Gracefully stop relay components (SSE + WebSocket). Does NOT stop the HTTP server. */
48: 	stop(): Promise<void>;
49: }
50: 
51: // ─── Full Stack Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
52: 
53: export interface RelayStackConfig {
54: 	port: number;
55: 	host?: string;
56: 	opencodeUrl: string;
57: 	pin?: string;
58: 	projectDir: string;
59: 	slug: string;
60: 	staticDir?: string;
61: 	/** TLS certificate and key for HTTPS mode */
62: 	tls?: { key: Buffer; cert: Buffer; caRoot?: string };
63: 	/** Session title for the initial session */
64: 	sessionTitle?: string;
65: 	/** Log function — defaults to console.log, pass () => {} to silence */
66: 	log?: (...args: unknown[]) => void;
67: 	/** Optional pre-initialized push notification manager */
68: 	pushManager?: PushNotificationManager;
69: }
70: 
71: // ─── Stack ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72: 
73: export interface RelayStack {
74: 	server: RelayServer;
75: 	wsHandler: WebSocketHandler;
76: 	sseConsumer: SSEConsumer;
77: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
78: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
79: 	translator: ReturnType<typeof createTranslator>;
80: 	permissionBridge: PermissionBridge;
81: 	questionBridge: QuestionBridge;
82: 
83: 	/** The port the HTTP server is actually listening on (useful when port=0) */
84: 	getPort(): number;
85: 	/** The base URL of the relay server */
86: 	getBaseUrl(): string;
87: 	/** Stop all components */
88: 	stop(): Promise<void>;
89: }
90: 
91: // ─── Create Per-Project Relay ────────────────────────────────────────────────
92: 
93: /**
94:  * Create a per-project relay that attaches to an existing HTTP server.
95:  *
96:  * Sets up all relay components (OpenCode client, SSE consumer, translator,
97:  * session manager, bridges, WebSocket handler) and wires the full event
98:  * pipeline. Does NOT create or manage an HTTP server — the caller owns it.
99:  *
100:  * Used by both `createRelayStack()` (for standalone/skeleton mode) and the
101:  * daemon (which has its own HTTP server).
102:  */
103: export async function createProjectRelay(
104: 	config: ProjectRelayConfig,
105: ): Promise<ProjectRelay> {
106: 	const log = config.log ?? console.log;
107: 
108: 	// ── Components ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
109: 
110: 	const client = new OpenCodeClient({
111: 		baseUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
112: 		directory: config.noServer ? config.projectDir : undefined,
113: 	});
114: 	const translator = createTranslator();
115: 	const permissionBridge = new PermissionBridge();
116: 	const questionBridge = new QuestionBridge();
117: 	const sessionMgr = new SessionManager({ client });
118: 
119: 	// ── Per-session event cache (identical to claude-relay's doSendAndRecord) ──
120: 	const cacheDir = join(
121: 		config.projectDir ?? process.cwd(),
122: 		".opencode-relay",
123: 		"sessions",
124: 	);
125: 	const messageCache = new MessageCache(cacheDir);
126: 	messageCache.loadFromDisk();
127: 
128: 	// Per-session overrides (agent, model, processing timeout)
129: 	const overrides = new SessionOverrides();
130: 
131: 	// ── PTY sessions with server-side scrollback (claude-relay architecture) ──
132: 	// Each active PTY gets one upstream WebSocket to OpenCode's /pty/:id/connect.
133: 	// Output is buffered server-side (50 KB FIFO per terminal) and broadcast to
134: 	// ALL browser clients. New clients get scrollback replayed on connect.
135: 	// Input from any browser client is forwarded to the shared upstream WS.
136: 	// PTYs persist across browser show/hide toggles — only closed on explicit
137: 	// pty_close (tab X button) or upstream disconnect.
138: 
139: 	const ptyManager = new PtyManager({ log });
140: 
141: 	async function connectPtyUpstream(
142: 		ptyId: string,
143: 		cursor: number = 0,
144: 	): Promise<void> {
145: 		// cursor=0 : replay entire PTY buffer (use for newly-created PTYs so the
146: 		//            initial shell prompt is delivered).
147: 		// cursor=-1: skip buffered data (use when reconnecting to an existing PTY
148: 		//            to avoid replaying stale escape sequences).
149: 		const httpUrl = new URL(
150: 			`/pty/${ptyId}/connect?cursor=${cursor}`,
151: 			config.opencodeUrl,
152: 		);
153: 		const wsUrl = httpUrl.toString().replace(/^http/, "ws");
154: 		const headers = client.getAuthHeaders();
155: 
156: 		return new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
157: 			const upstream = new WebSocketClass(wsUrl, {
158: 				headers,
159: 				// Disable per-message compression for PTY connections.
160: 				// Bun's WS may negotiate permessage-deflate with Node's ws, and
161: 				// decompression bugs or flush mismatches can corrupt binary PTY data.
162: 				perMessageDeflate: false,
163: 				// Skip UTF-8 validation for TEXT frames. Terminal data can contain
164: 				// 8-bit characters (C1 control codes) that are valid in terminal
165: 				// protocols but technically invalid in strict UTF-8. Without this,
166: 				// the ws library would close the connection on such frames.
167: 				skipUTF8Validation: true,
168: 			});
169: 
170: 			// Register session BEFORE any events can fire. This mirrors the
171: 			// original pattern where the PtySession object was created before
172: 			// the WebSocket — prevents the race condition where OpenCode sends
173: 			// replay data in the same TCP segment as the upgrade response
174: 			// (Node.js ws processes both synchronously).
175: 			ptyManager.registerSession(ptyId, upstream as unknown as PtyUpstream);
176: 			let connected = false;
177: 
178: 			const connectTimeout = setTimeout(() => {
179: 				// Remove the session on timeout — it was never successfully opened
180: 				ptyManager.closeSession(ptyId);
181: 				reject(new Error(`Timed out connecting to PTY ${ptyId}`));
182: 			}, 10_000);
183: 
184: 			// Install message handler BEFORE "open" fires.
185: 			upstream.on("message", (data: RawData) => {
186: 				// OpenCode sends cursor metadata as binary: 0x00 + JSON {"cursor":N}.
187: 				// Always convert to Buffer first and check byte 0.
188: 				let buf: Buffer;
189: 				if (Buffer.isBuffer(data)) {
190: 					buf = data;
191: 				} else if (typeof data === "string") {
192: 					buf = Buffer.from(data);
193: 				} else {
194: 					buf = Buffer.from(data as ArrayBuffer);
195: 				}
196: 
197: 				if (buf.length > 0 && buf[0] === 0x00) return;
198: 				const text = buf.toString();
199: 				if (!text) return;
200: 
201: 				// Buffer scrollback via PtyManager (FIFO, 50 KB cap)
202: 				ptyManager.appendScrollback(ptyId, text);
203: 
204: 				// Broadcast to all browser clients
205: 				wsHandler.broadcast({ type: "pty_output", ptyId, data: text });
206: 			});
207: 
208: 			upstream.on("close", () => {
209: 				ptyManager.markExited(ptyId, 0);
210: 				// Only broadcast pty_exited if the session is still tracked.
211: 				// closeSession deletes + broadcasts pty_deleted, so we must
212: 				// not also broadcast pty_exited for the same PTY.  Similarly,
213: 				// if connectPtyUpstream failed the session was never stored.
214: 				if (ptyManager.hasSession(ptyId)) {
215: 					wsHandler.broadcast({
216: 						type: "pty_exited",
217: 						ptyId,
218: 						exitCode: 0,
219: 					});
220: 				}
221: 				log(`   [pty] Upstream closed: ${ptyId}`);
222: 			});
223: 
224: 			upstream.on("error", (err: Error) => {
225: 				if (!connected) {
226: 					clearTimeout(connectTimeout);
227: 					// Remove the failed session
228: 					ptyManager.closeSession(ptyId);
229: 					reject(err);
230: 				}
231: 				log(`   [pty] Upstream error ${ptyId}: ${err.message}`);
232: 			});
233: 
234: 			upstream.on("open", () => {
235: 				connected = true;
236: 				clearTimeout(connectTimeout);
237: 				resolve();
238: 			});
239: 		});
240: 	}
241: 
242: 	// ── Health check ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
243: 
244: 	await client.getHealth();
245: 	log(`   ✓ OpenCode is reachable at ${config.opencodeUrl}`);
246: 
247: 	// ── Session ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
248: 
249: 	const sessionId = await sessionMgr.initialize(
250: 		config.sessionTitle ?? "Relay Session",
251: 	);
252: 	log(`   ✓ Using session: ${sessionId}`);
253: 
254: 	// ── WebSocket handler ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
255: 
256: 	const wsHandler = new WebSocketHandler(
257: 		config.noServer ? null : config.httpServer,
258: 		{
259: 			// In noServer mode, the daemon's upgrade handler checks auth before
260: 			// calling handleUpgrade(). Skip verifyClient to avoid double auth.
261: 			verifyClient: config.noServer ? undefined : config.verifyClient,
262: 		},
263: 	);
264: 
265: 	const clientInitDeps: ClientInitDeps = {
266: 		wsHandler,
267: 		client,
268: 		sessionMgr,
269: 		messageCache,
270: 		overrides,
271: 		ptyManager,
272: 		permissionBridge,
273: 		questionBridge,
274: 		log,
275: 	};
276: 
277: 	wsHandler.on("client_connected", ({ clientId }) => {
278: 		log(`   [ws] Client connected: ${clientId}`);
279: 		handleClientConnected(clientInitDeps, clientId).catch((err) =>
280: 			log(
281: 				`   [ws] Client init failed for ${clientId}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`,
282: 			),
283: 		);
284: 	});
285: 
286: 	wsHandler.on("client_disconnected", ({ clientId }) => {
287: 		log(`   [ws] Client disconnected: ${clientId}`);
288: 	});
289: 
290: 	// ── SSE consumer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
291: 
292: 	const sseConsumer = new SSEConsumer({
293: 		baseUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
294: 		authHeaders: client.getAuthHeaders(),
295: 	});
296: 
297: 	// ── Wire session manager → WebSocket ────────────────────────────────────
298: 
299: 	sessionMgr.on("broadcast", (msg) => {
300: 		// Augment session_switched with cached events (combined protocol)
301: 		if (msg.type === "session_switched") {
302: 			const switchId = (msg as { id?: string }).id;
303: 			if (switchId) {
304: 				const events = messageCache.getEvents(switchId);
305: 				const hasChatContent =
306: 					events?.some(
307: 						(e) => e.type === "user_message" || e.type === "delta",
308: 					) ?? false;
309: 				if (events && hasChatContent) {
310: 					wsHandler.broadcast({ ...msg, events });
311: 					return;
312: 				}
313: 			}
314: 		}
315: 		wsHandler.broadcast(msg);
316: 	});
317: 	sessionMgr.on("session_changed", async ({ sessionId: sid }) => {
318: 		translator.reset();
319: 		// Rebuild translator state from REST API so it knows which parts already
320: 		// exist in the session we're switching to (prevents duplicate tool_start).
321: 		try {
322: 			const messages = await client.getMessages(sid);
323: 			const parts: Array<{
324: 				parts?: Array<{
325: 					id: string;
326: 					type: PartType;
327: 					state?: { status?: ToolStatus };
328: 				}>;
329: 			}> = messages.map((m) => ({
330: 				parts: (m as { parts?: unknown[] }).parts as
331: 					| Array<{
332: 							id: string;
333: 							type: PartType;
334: 							state?: { status?: ToolStatus };
335: 					  }>
336: 					| undefined,
337: 			}));
338: 			translator.rebuildStateFromHistory(parts);
339: 		} catch {
340: 			// Non-fatal — translator will treat all parts as new
341: 		}
342: 	});
343: 
344: 	// ── Handle incoming messages from browser ───────────────────────────────
345: 	// Messages are serialized: each handler completes before the next starts.
346: 	// Without this, concurrent async handlers (e.g. new_session + switch_session)
347: 	// race — createSession's broadcast can arrive on the client AFTER
348: 	// a switch_session reply, clearing messages the user just switched to.
349: 
350: 	const handlerDeps: HandlerDeps = {
351: 		wsHandler,
352: 		client,
353: 		sessionMgr,
354: 		messageCache,
355: 		permissionBridge,
356: 		questionBridge,
357: 		overrides,
358: 		ptyManager,
359: 		config,
360: 		log,
361: 		connectPtyUpstream,
362: 	};
363: 
364: 	let _messageQueue = Promise.resolve();
365: 
366: 	wsHandler.on("message", ({ clientId, handler, payload }) => {
367: 		_messageQueue = _messageQueue.then(async () => {
368: 			try {
369: 				await dispatchMessage(handlerDeps, clientId, handler, payload);
370: 			} catch (err) {
371: 				log(`   [ws] Error handling ${handler}:`, formatErrorDetail(err));
372: 				wsHandler.sendTo(
373: 					clientId,
374: 					RelayError.fromCaught(err, "HANDLER_ERROR").toMessage(),
375: 				);
376: 			}
377: 		});
378: 	});
379: 
380: 	// ── SSE event wiring (translate → filter → cache → broadcast) ──────────
381: 
382: 	wireSSEConsumer(
383: 		{
384: 			translator,
385: 			sessionMgr,
386: 			messageCache,
387: 			permissionBridge,
388: 			questionBridge,
389: 			overrides,
390: 			wsHandler,
391: 			pushManager: config.pushManager,
392: 			log,
393: 		},
394: 		sseConsumer,
395: 	);
396: 
397: 	await sseConsumer.connect();
398: 
399: 	// ── Permission/question timeout checks ──────────────────────────────────
400: 
401: 	const timeoutTimer = setInterval(() => {
402: 		const timedOutPerms = permissionBridge.checkTimeouts();
403: 		for (const id of timedOutPerms) {
404: 			wsHandler.broadcast({
405: 				type: "permission_resolved",
406: 				requestId: id,
407: 				decision: "timeout",
408: 			});
409: 		}
410: 		const timedOutQuestions = questionBridge.checkTimeouts();
411: 		for (const id of timedOutQuestions) {
412: 			questionBridge.remove(id);
413: 			wsHandler.broadcast({ type: "ask_user_resolved", toolId: id });
414: 		}
415: 	}, 30_000);
416: 
417: 	// Don't let the timer keep the process alive
418: 	if (
419: 		timeoutTimer &&
420: 		typeof timeoutTimer === "object" &&
421: 		"unref" in timeoutTimer
422: 	) {
423: 		timeoutTimer.unref();
424: 	}
425: 
426: 	// ── Return project relay ────────────────────────────────────────────────
427: 
428: 	return {
429: 		wsHandler,
430: 		sseConsumer,
431: 		client,
432: 		sessionMgr,
433: 		translator,
434: 		permissionBridge,
435: 		questionBridge,
436: 
437: 		async stop() {
438: 			clearInterval(timeoutTimer);
439: 			overrides.dispose();
440: 			ptyManager.closeAll();
441: 			await sseConsumer.disconnect();
442: 			wsHandler.close();
443: 		},
444: 	};
445: }
446: 
447: // ─── Create Full Stack (Server + Relay) ─────────────────────────────────────
448: 
449: /**
450:  * Create a full relay stack with its own HTTP server.
451:  *
452:  * Creates a RelayServer, registers the project, starts the server, then
453:  * delegates to `createProjectRelay()` for all relay wiring. Used by
454:  * skeleton.ts for standalone operation.
455:  */
456: export async function createRelayStack(
457: 	config: RelayStackConfig,
458: ): Promise<RelayStack> {
459: 	const log = config.log ?? console.log;
460: 
461: 	// ── Push notification manager ────────────────────────────────────────────
462: 
463: 	let pushMgr: PushNotificationManager | undefined = config.pushManager;
464: 	if (!pushMgr) {
465: 		try {
466: 			const { PushNotificationManager } = await import("./push.js");
467: 			pushMgr = new PushNotificationManager();
468: 			await pushMgr.init();
469: 		} catch {
470: 			pushMgr = undefined;
471: 		}
472: 	}
473: 
474: 	// ── HTTP server ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
475: 
476: 	const server = new RelayServer({
477: 		port: config.port,
478: 		host: config.host,
479: 		pin: config.pin || undefined,
480: 		staticDir: config.staticDir,
481: 		tls: config.tls,
482: 		pushManager: pushMgr,
483: 	});
484: 
485: 	server.addProject({
486: 		slug: config.slug,
487: 		directory: config.projectDir,
488: 		title: config.slug,
489: 	});
490: 
491: 	await server.start();
492: 
493: 	const maybeServer = server.getHttpServer();
494: 	if (!maybeServer) {
495: 		throw new Error("HTTP server not available after start()");
496: 	}
497: 	// Assign to a fresh const so TypeScript narrows to non-null in closures.
498: 	const httpServer = maybeServer;
499: 
500: 	// ── Multi-project relay management ──────────────────────────────────────
501: 	// All relays use noServer mode. A single upgrade handler routes WebSocket
502: 	// connections to the correct relay by URL path (/ws → initial, /p/{slug}/ws → project).
503: 	// This matches the daemon pattern and allows dynamic project addition.
504: 
505: 	const relays = new Map<string, ProjectRelay>();
506: 	const pendingSlugs = new Set<string>();
507: 
508: 	const getProjectList = () =>
509: 		server.getProjects().map((p) => ({
510: 			slug: p.slug,
511: 			title: p.title,
512: 			directory: p.directory,
513: 		}));
514: 
515: 	/** Create a new project relay and register it. */
516: 	async function addProjectRelay(
517: 		directory: string,
518: 	): Promise<{ slug: string; title: string; directory: string }> {
519: 		// Expand ~ and resolve to absolute path
520: 		if (directory.startsWith("~/") || directory === "~") {
521: 			directory = directory.replace("~", homedir());
522: 		}
523: 		directory = resolve(directory);
524: 
525: 		// Check if directory is already registered
526: 		for (const p of server.getProjects()) {
527: 			if (p.directory === directory) {
528: 				return { slug: p.slug, title: p.title, directory: p.directory };
529: 			}
530: 		}
531: 
532: 		// Validate directory exists on disk
533: 		const dirStat = await stat(directory).catch(() => null);
534: 		if (!dirStat?.isDirectory()) {
535: 			throw new Error(
536: 				dirStat
537: 					? `Not a directory: ${directory}`
538: 					: `Directory does not exist: ${directory}`,
539: 			);
540: 		}
541: 
542: 		const existingSlugs = new Set(relays.keys());
543: 		const slug = generateSlug(directory, existingSlugs);
544: 		const parts = directory.replace(/\\/g, "/").split("/").filter(Boolean);
545: 		const title = parts[parts.length - 1] ?? "project";
546: 
547: 		// Guard against concurrent creation for the same slug
548: 		if (relays.has(slug) || pendingSlugs.has(slug)) {
549: 			const existing = relays.get(slug);
550: 			if (existing) return { slug, title, directory };
551: 			throw new Error(`Relay for ${directory} is still being created`);
552: 		}
553: 
554: 		pendingSlugs.add(slug);
555: 		try {
556: 			// Create relay FIRST — if this throws, nothing is registered
557: 			const newRelay = await createProjectRelay({
558: 				httpServer,
559: 				opencodeUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
560: 				projectDir: directory,
561: 				slug,
562: 				noServer: true,
563: 				sessionTitle: config.sessionTitle,
564: 				log,
565: 				getProjects: getProjectList,
566: 				addProject: addProjectRelay,
567: 				pushManager: pushMgr,
568: 			});
569: 
570: 			// Only register AFTER relay is successfully created
571: 			relays.set(slug, newRelay);
572: 			server.addProject({ slug, directory, title });
573: 
574: 			log(`   [project] Added: ${title} (${slug}) → ${directory}`);
575: 		} catch (err) {
576: 			// Clean up on failure — no zombie entries
577: 			relays.delete(slug);
578: 			log(`   [project] Failed to add ${directory}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`);
579: 			throw err;
580: 		} finally {
581: 			pendingSlugs.delete(slug);
582: 		}
583: 
584: 		return { slug, title, directory };
585: 	}
586: 
587: 	// ── Initial project relay ───────────────────────────────────────────────
588: 
589: 	const relay = await createProjectRelay({
590: 		httpServer,
591: 		opencodeUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
592: 		projectDir: config.projectDir,
593: 		slug: config.slug,
594: 		sessionTitle: config.sessionTitle,
595: 		log,
596: 		noServer: true,
597: 		getProjects: getProjectList,
598: 		addProject: addProjectRelay,
599: 		pushManager: pushMgr,
600: 	});
601: 	relays.set(config.slug, relay);
602: 
603: 	// ── WebSocket upgrade handler ───────────────────────────────────────────
604: 	// Routes connections by URL: /p/{slug}/ws → project relay, /ws → initial relay.
605: 	// Also checks auth when a PIN is configured (fixes pre-existing gap where
606: 	// standalone WS connections bypassed PIN auth).
607: 
608: 	httpServer.on("upgrade", (req, socket, head) => {
609: 		// Auth check (mirrors server.ts private checkAuth)
610: 		const auth = server.getAuth();
611: 		if (auth.hasPin()) {
612: 			const cookies = parseCookies(req.headers.cookie ?? "");
613: 			const sessionCookie = cookies.relay_session;
614: 			const cookieOk = sessionCookie
615: 				? auth.validateCookie(sessionCookie)
616: 				: false;
617: 			if (!cookieOk) {
618: 				const pinHeader = req.headers["x-relay-pin"];
619: 				const pinOk =
620: 					typeof pinHeader === "string" &&
621: 					auth.authenticate(pinHeader, getClientIp(req)).ok;
622: 				if (!pinOk) {
623: 					socket.destroy();
624: 					return;
625: 				}
626: 			}
627: 		}
628: 
629: 		// Route /p/{slug}/ws → project relay
630: 		const projectMatch = req.url?.match(/^\/p\/([^/]+)\/ws(?:\?|$)/);
631: 		if (projectMatch) {
632: 			const target = relays.get(projectMatch[1]!);
633: 			if (target) {
634: 				target.wsHandler.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head);
635: 			} else {
636: 				socket.destroy();
637: 			}
638: 			return;
639: 		}
640: 
641: 		// Route /ws → initial relay
642: 		if (req.url === "/ws" || req.url?.startsWith("/ws?")) {
643: 			relay.wsHandler.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head);
644: 			return;
645: 		}
646: 
647: 		socket.destroy();
648: 	});
649: 
650: 	const urls = server.getUrls();
651: 	log(`   ✓ Server listening: ${urls.local}`);
652: 
653: 	return {
654: 		server,
655: 		wsHandler: relay.wsHandler,
656: 		sseConsumer: relay.sseConsumer,
657: 		client: relay.client,
658: 		sessionMgr: relay.sessionMgr,
659: 		translator: relay.translator,
660: 		permissionBridge: relay.permissionBridge,
661: 		questionBridge: relay.questionBridge,
662: 
663: 		getPort() {
664: 			const addr = httpServer.address();
665: 			if (typeof addr === "object" && addr) return addr.port;
666: 			return config.port;
667: 		},
668: 
669: 		getBaseUrl() {
670: 			const addr = httpServer.address();
671: 			const port = typeof addr === "object" && addr ? addr.port : config.port;
672: 			const protocol = config.tls ? "https" : "http";
673: 			return `${protocol}://127.0.0.1:${port}`;
674: 		},
675: 
676: 		async stop() {
677: 			for (const r of relays.values()) {
678: 				try {
679: 					await r.stop();
680: 				} catch {
681: 					/* ignore */
682: 				}
683: 			}
684: 			relays.clear();
685: 			await server.stop();
686: 		},
687: 	};
688: }

<content>1: // ─── ToolContentStore ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // In-memory store for full tool result content (before truncation).
3: // Keyed by tool ID, with optional session-based grouping for bulk cleanup.
4: // Evicts oldest entries when over capacity.
5: 
6: interface Entry {
7: 	toolId: string;
8: 	content: string;
9: 	sessionId?: string;
10: 	/** Monotonic insertion order for eviction. */
11: 	order: number;
12: }
13: 
14: export class ToolContentStore {
15: 	private readonly entries = new Map<string, Entry>();
16: 	private readonly maxEntries: number;
17: 	private orderCounter = 0;
18: 
19: 	constructor(maxEntries = 500) {
20: 		this.maxEntries = maxEntries;
21: 	}
22: 
23: 	/** Store full content for a tool ID, optionally associated with a session. */
24: 	store(toolId: string, content: string, sessionId?: string): void {
25: 		// If overwriting, remove old entry first (don't double-count)
26: 		this.entries.delete(toolId);
27: 
28: 		this.entries.set(toolId, {
29: 			toolId,
30: 			content,
31: 			sessionId,
32: 			order: this.orderCounter++,
33: 		});
34: 
35: 		// Evict oldest if over capacity
36: 		while (this.entries.size > this.maxEntries) {
37: 			this.evictOldest();
38: 		}
39: 	}
40: 
41: 	/** Retrieve stored content by tool ID. */
42: 	get(toolId: string): string | undefined {
43: 		return this.entries.get(toolId)?.content;
44: 	}
45: 
46: 	/** Remove all entries associated with a session. */
47: 	clearSession(sessionId: string): void {
48: 		for (const [toolId, entry] of this.entries) {
49: 			if (entry.sessionId === sessionId) {
50: 				this.entries.delete(toolId);
51: 			}
52: 		}
53: 	}
54: 
55: 	/** Number of stored entries. */
56: 	get size(): number {
57: 		return this.entries.size;
58: 	}
59: 
60: 	/** Evict the entry with the lowest order (oldest). */
61: 	private evictOldest(): void {
62: 		let oldestKey: string | undefined;
63: 		let oldestOrder = Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY;
64: 
65: 		for (const [key, entry] of this.entries) {
66: 			if (entry.order < oldestOrder) {
67: 				oldestOrder = entry.order;
68: 				oldestKey = key;
69: 			}
70: 		}
71: 
72: 		if (oldestKey !== undefined) {
73: 			this.entries.delete(oldestKey);
74: 		}
75: 	}
76: }

<content>1: // ─── Content Truncation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Utilities for truncating large tool result content before relay to clients.
3: // Full content is preserved separately in ToolContentStore for on-demand fetch.
4: 
5: import type { RelayMessage } from "./shared-types.js";
6: 
7: /** Default truncation threshold in characters (~50KB). */
8: export const TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD = 50_000;
9: 
10: /** Suffix appended to truncated content. */
11: const TRUNCATION_SUFFIX = "\n\n[truncated]";
12: 
13: export interface TruncateResult {
14: 	content: string;
15: 	isTruncated: boolean;
16: 	fullContentLength?: number;
17: }
18: 
19: /**
20:  * Truncate content if it exceeds the threshold.
21:  * Returns the (possibly truncated) content, a flag, and the original length
22:  * when truncated.
23:  */
24: export function truncateContent(
25: 	content: string,
26: 	threshold = TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD,
27: ): TruncateResult {
28: 	if (content.length <= threshold) {
29: 		return { content, isTruncated: false };
30: 	}
31: 
32: 	// When the threshold is smaller than the suffix, just slice to threshold.
33: 	const suffixFits = threshold > TRUNCATION_SUFFIX.length;
34: 	const truncated = suffixFits
35: 		? content.slice(0, threshold - TRUNCATION_SUFFIX.length) + TRUNCATION_SUFFIX
36: 		: content.slice(0, threshold);
37: 
38: 	return {
39: 		content: truncated,
40: 		isTruncated: true,
41: 		fullContentLength: content.length,
42: 	};
43: }
44: 
45: /** A tool_result RelayMessage (narrowed). */
46: type ToolResultMessage = Extract<RelayMessage, { type: "tool_result" }>;
47: 
48: export interface TruncateToolResultResult {
49: 	/** The (possibly truncated) tool_result message. */
50: 	truncated: ToolResultMessage;
51: 	/** The full original content, or undefined if no truncation occurred. */
52: 	fullContent: string | undefined;
53: }
54: 
55: /**
56:  * Process a tool_result RelayMessage, truncating its content if over threshold.
57:  * Returns the modified message and the full content (if truncated).
58:  */
59: export function truncateToolResult(
60: 	msg: ToolResultMessage,
61: ): TruncateToolResultResult {
62: 	const { content, isTruncated, fullContentLength } = truncateContent(
63: 		msg.content,
64: 	);
65: 
66: 	if (!isTruncated) {
67: 		return { truncated: msg, fullContent: undefined };
68: 	}
69: 
70: 	return {
71: 		truncated: {
72: 			...msg,
73: 			content,
74: 			isTruncated: true,
75: 			fullContentLength,
76: 		},
77: 		fullContent: msg.content,
78: 	};
79: }

5: export async function handleGetFileList(
6: 	deps: HandlerDeps,
7: 	clientId: string,

15: 		entries: files as Array<{
16: 			name: string;
17: 			type: "file" | "directory";
18: 			size?: number;
19: 		}>,
20: 	});
21: }
22: 
23: export async function handleGetFileContent(
24: 	deps: HandlerDeps,
25: 	clientId: string,
26: 	payload: Record<string, unknown>,
27: ): Promise<void> {
28: 	const filePath = String(payload.path ?? "");
29: 	if (filePath) {
30: 		const result = await deps.client.getFileContent(filePath);
31: 		deps.wsHandler.sendTo(clientId, {
32: 			type: "file_content",
33: 			path: filePath,
34: 			content: (result as { content: string }).content ?? "",
35: 			binary: (result as { binary?: boolean }).binary,
36: 		});
37: 	}
